heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 1st among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 21 days so far.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: A heatwave started on 2026-07-01 and it has so far lasted for 23 days.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves; the long-term yearly average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (220°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will remain comfortably hot. Outside; hot temperatures throughout the day. Also, a strong muggy feeling will dominate and winds will be practically absent. Showers, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and bright sunshine will dominate the day. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
